selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 2.5% and 20-day momentum is 8.3%.
Trend quality is supported by 70% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 4.8% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.5%, downside volatility near 1.1%, and a recent max drawdown of 3.3%.
Volume confirmation is -4.5% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 1.5% versus SPY over 5 days and -1.9% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.67, confidence 0.69, and 0.6 weighted articles across 1 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.67, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 1 sources, 0.6 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.88, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.69.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.01).
Overall model score is 0.099 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Visa: Buy The Next Sell-Off, Not The Rally (NYSE:V)
news_evidence.llm_reason: Company-focused commentary emphasizing strong Q2 results (20% EPS growth, robust volumes) and recommending buying on pullbacks. High relevance and moderately material for short-term trading because it reinforces positive earnings momentum and could support buying interest during dips.

news_evidence.title: Visa: Still An Exceptional Business, But Risks Are Rising (NYSE:V)
news_evidence.llm_reason: Analyst piece focused on Visa that explicitly weighs Q2 results against rising macro risks and rates the stock Hold. Directly relevant to Visa and can influence short-term sentiment, but contains analysis/opinion rather than new company disclosures — moderate materiality for 1–10 day horizon as it may dampen enthusiasm but not likely to move fundamentals.

news_evidence.title: Visa: How Visa Is Turning Disruption Into Opportunity (NYSE:V)
news_evidence.llm_reason: Positive analysis highlighting Q2 revenue +17%, EPS +20% and strategic shift to value‑added services (~30% of revenue). Highly relevant and relatively material for the near term as it frames Visa's growth drivers and may bolster bullish sentiment among investors assessing earnings implications.
